title: Home Lab: SIEM Deployment & Triage Playbook
overview: Build a local logging environment to simulate adversary behavior and practice detection engineering.

journeySteps.name: Infrastructure Setup
journeySteps.instruction: Deploy an ELK Stack (Elasticsearch, Logstash, Kibana) or Splunk Free locally using Docker Compose.
journeySteps.learningOutcome: Practical experience with containerized logging infrastructure and data ingestion.

journeySteps.name: Data Ingestion & Parsing
journeySteps.instruction: Configure Sysmon on a Windows VM and forward Windows Event Logs to your SIEM.
journeySteps.learningOutcome: Understanding of endpoint telemetry and log normalization.

journeySteps.name: Simulate & Detect
journeySteps.instruction: Use Atomic Red Team to simulate credential dumping (e.g., Mimikatz). Write a detection rule for the generated logs.
journeySteps.learningOutcome: Hands-on threat hunting and detection rule creation.

title: Zero Trust Architecture: Edge Security Simulation
overview: Design and implement a simulated Zero Trust boundary using open-source tools to secure remote access.

journeySteps.name: Identity Provider Setup
journeySteps.instruction: Configure a free tier Okta or Entra ID tenant with multi-factor authentication (MFA) enabled.
journeySteps.learningOutcome: Core understanding of IAM and IdP integration.

journeySteps.name: Deploy Identity-Aware Proxy
journeySteps.instruction: Set up Cloudflare Tunnels or an open-source IAP (like Pomerium) in front of a dummy web application.
journeySteps.learningOutcome: Replacing traditional VPNs with context-aware access controls.

journeySteps.name: Granular Policy Creation
journeySteps.instruction: Write policies that restrict access based on user role and device posture (e.g., block access from non-compliant IPs).
journeySteps.learningOutcome: Implementing Least Privilege in a modern edge environment.

title: Shift-Left: Automated Security in CI/CD
overview: Build a secure software supply chain by integrating automated security gates into a GitHub Actions pipeline.

journeySteps.name: Vulnerable App Baseline
journeySteps.instruction: Fork a deliberately vulnerable application (e.g., OWASP Juice Shop) into a new GitHub repository.
journeySteps.learningOutcome: Understanding common web vulnerabilities and Git workflows.

journeySteps.name: SAST & Secret Scanning
journeySteps.instruction: Implement GitHub Actions to run Semgrep (SAST) and TruffleHog (Secret Scanning) on every Pull Request.

journeySteps.name: Container Security
journeySteps.instruction: Add a pipeline step to build a Docker image and scan it with Trivy before pushing to a registry. Block the build on 'CRITICAL' findings.
journeySteps.learningOutcome: Ensuring secure container orchestration and artifact integrity.

title: Cloud Workload Protection & Auditing
overview: Deploy cloud infrastructure using Terraform and audit it for misconfigurations using CSPM tooling.

journeySteps.name: Insecure Infrastructure via IaC
journeySteps.instruction: Write a Terraform script to deploy an AWS S3 bucket with public read access and an EC2 instance with an open SSH port.

journeySteps.name: Automated Auditing
journeySteps.instruction: Run Checkov or Prowler against your Terraform configuration to identify security misconfigurations.
journeySteps.learningOutcome: Using CSPM tools to assess compliance against frameworks like CIS benchmarks.

journeySteps.name: Remediation & Least Privilege
journeySteps.instruction: Refactor the Terraform code to enforce S3 Block Public Access and attach an IAM instance profile utilizing least privilege.
journeySteps.learningOutcome: Practical remediation of cloud security risks.

title: Threat Intel Sandbox & OSINT Investigation
overview: Safely analyze a malicious payload and map adversary techniques to the MITRE ATT&CK framework.

journeySteps.name: Safe Sandbox Setup
journeySteps.instruction: Deploy a heavily isolated Windows Flare-VM or REMnux environment. Ensure host-only networking.
journeySteps.learningOutcome: Establishing safe environments for handling live malware.

journeySteps.name: Static & Dynamic Analysis
journeySteps.instruction: Obtain a benign test file or defanged malware sample. Use tools like Pestudio (static) and Procmon/Wireshark (dynamic) to observe behavior.
journeySteps.learningOutcome: Identifying Indicators of Compromise (IoCs) and behavioral anomalies.

journeySteps.name: OSINT & MITRE Mapping
journeySteps.instruction: Take the discovered file hashes/IPs and pivot using VirusTotal or AlienVault OTX. Map the observed behaviors to specific MITRE ATT&CK T-codes.
journeySteps.learningOutcome: Generating actionable threat intelligence and contextualizing adversary methods.
